title: Microscopic origin of Bekenstein-Hawking entropy
statement: A black hole carries entropy S = k_B c^3 A / (4 G hbar) — one quarter of its horizon area in Planck units (Bekenstein 1973; Hawking 1975). In ordinary statistical mechanics, entropy counts microstates. What are the microstates of a black hole? String theory (Strominger-Vafa 1996) reproduced S = A/4 by counting D-brane states for special supersymmetric, extremal (and near-extremal) black holes. A microscopic state-counting for a generic astrophysical (e.g. Kerr) black hole, and a framework-independent understanding of why the answer is universally A/4, remain open.

originators.name: Jacob Bekenstein
originators.note: 1972-73 proposed that black holes carry entropy proportional to horizon area.

originators.name: Stephen Hawking
originators.note: 1975 fixed the coefficient to 1/4 via the radiation temperature (the first law).

ranking.rationale: Deep (the meaning of A/4 touches quantum gravity and holography), old (1973), but partially solved: string theory counts microstates exactly for SUSY/extremal cases. The general (Kerr) case and a universal explanation are open.

key_ideas: S = A/4 in Planck units is universal across black-hole types — a strong hint about quantum gravity.
The area law motivated the holographic principle ('t Hooft, Susskind): degrees of freedom scale with area, not volume.
Strominger-Vafa counted Bogomol'nyi-Prasad-Sommerfield (BPS) D-brane microstates and matched A/4 exactly for extremal charged black holes.
Loop quantum gravity counts spin-network punctures of the horizon, fixing A/4 via the Barbero-Immirzi parameter.
Logarithmic corrections to S (Sen and others) provide cross-checks between approaches.
Open: a microscopic count for a generic (non-extremal, rotating, astrophysical) black hole and a framework-independent derivation of the 1/4.

approaches.name: String theory / D-brane counting
approaches.summary: Count BPS microstates of brane bound states; matches A/4 for SUSY extremal and near-extremal black holes.

approaches.name: Loop quantum gravity horizon counting
approaches.summary: Count quantum-geometry states puncturing the horizon; fixes A/4 via the Barbero-Immirzi parameter.

approaches.name: Euclidean / Noether-charge (Wald) entropy
approaches.summary: Entropy as a Noether charge of the horizon-generating Killing field; generalizes A/4 to higher-curvature gravity.

approaches.name: Conformal symmetry near the horizon (Cardy)
approaches.summary: Near-horizon conformal symmetry fixes the state count via Cardy's formula, independent of microscopic details.

papers.title: Black Holes and Entropy
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D 7, 2333 — entropy proportional to horizon area.

papers.title: Particle Creation by Black Holes
papers.note: Commun. Math. Phys. 43, 199 — fixes S = A/4.

papers.title: Microscopic Origin of the Bekenstein-Hawking Entropy
papers.note: D-brane microstate counting matching A/4 for extremal black holes.

papers.title: Black Hole Entropy is the Noether Charge
Black Hole Entropy from Conformal Field Theory in Any Dimension

physicists.name: Jacob Bekenstein
physicists.contribution: Proposed black-hole entropy proportional to horizon area.

physicists.name: Stephen Hawking
physicists.contribution: Fixed the 1/4 coefficient via the radiation temperature.

physicists.name: Andrew Strominger
physicists.contribution: String-theory microstate counting; near-horizon conformal symmetry.

physicists.name: Ashoke Sen
physicists.contribution: Precision entropy, logarithmic corrections, quantum entropy function.

physicists.name: Steven Carlip
physicists.contribution: Near-horizon conformal (Cardy) approach to universality of A/4.

simulator_angle: The horizon area (and thus S = A/4) for Kerr is exactly what the simulator's thermo/hawking.hpp computes (horizon_area_factor, entropy functions); the catalogue documents what that number means and why its microscopic origin is unsolved.

metadata.coverage_note: Seed: 5 key references (Strominger-Vafa, Wald, Carlip with arXiv ids; Bekenstein/Hawking as notes) + 8 physicists. Aspiration up to 25 papers, to be expanded.
